85.reason: [The line number starts at 1, but it's incremented before processing the first line, causing all line numbers to be off by one.]

172.reason: [If str is "&" or "|", symbol.at(str) will return ANDOP or OROP, not AND or OR. But then the code overrides cur_type to AND/OR after possibly printing an error, leading to inconsistent token type and output.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.125 , reason:  [If str is "&" or "|", symbol.at(str) will return ANDOP or OROP, not AND or OR. But then the code overrides cur_type to AND/OR after possibly printing an error, leading to inconsistent token type and output.]

174.reason: [This assignment happens after printOut_for_error(), but printOut() is called later with this modified type, which may output AND/OR instead of the erroneous single &/| as a valid token, contradicting the requirement to report error 'a' and not produce a token.]

179.reason: [Same problem: symbol.at("&") returns ANDOP, but then cur_type is reassigned to AND after error reporting.]

185.reason: [This outputs a token even for erroneous single '&' or '|', which violates the spec: erroneous programs should only output to error.txt, not lexer.txt.]
